prompt: Which statement best describes when an application is a good fit to build natively on ServiceNow?
choices: When it manages structured records, tasks, approvals, and reporting on the platform data model
When it requires a proprietary desktop-only kernel driver
When it must avoid all use of tables and forms
When it exclusively renders 3D game graphics
explanation: Applications centered on structured data, tasks, approvals, and reporting align with the platform's tables, workflow, and UI capabilities.

prompt: What is the purpose of an Application File in a scoped application?
choices: It is any configuration record (table, script, form, etc.) that belongs to and is tracked by the application
It is a binary attachment stored on the sys_user table
It is a temporary cache cleared on logout
It is a MID Server credential
explanation: Application files are the configuration records (tables, business rules, forms, and more) associated with and packaged by an application.

prompt: You want a module in your application to open a filtered list of records by default. What do you configure on the module?
choices: Set the module link type to List and define the table and default filter conditions
Delete the application menu
Change the table to global scope
Convert the module into an access control
explanation: A module of link type List targets a table and can apply default filter conditions to show a pre-filtered list.

prompt: Which UI element lets you group related fields into collapsible sections on a form?
choices: Form sections (and form layout) configured with the Form Designer
Access control rules on the table
Scheduled jobs that run nightly
Transform maps for imports
explanation: Form sections, arranged with the Form Designer or Form Layout, group fields into organized, collapsible areas on a form.

prompt: To add or remove fields shown on a form for a table, which tool do you use?
choices: Form Designer (Configure > Form Design/Form Layout)
The REST API Explorer
The Import Set loader
The Flow Designer trigger panel
explanation: Form Designer / Form Layout lets developers add, remove, and arrange fields displayed on a form.
